Adrenal insufficiency.Definition: A disorder characterized by the adrenal cortex not producing enough cortisol, and/or aldosterone.
Adrenal insufficiency.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated

Diabetes mellitus.Definition: A disorder characterized by high blood glucose level due to the body's inability to metabolize carbohydrates.
Diabetes mellitus.Grade 3: Fasting blood glucose >250 - 500 mg/dL (>13.9 - 27.8 mmol/L); hospitalization indicated
Diabetes mellitus.Grade 4: Fasting blood glucose >500 mg/dL (>27.8 mmol/L); life-threatening consequences

Hypophysitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation of the pituitary gland.
Hypophysitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypophysitis.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated
Hypophysitis.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated

Hypopituitarism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in production of pituitary hormones.
Hypopituitarism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic or mild symptoms; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypopituitarism.Grade 2: Moderate; minimal, local or noninvasive intervention indicated
Hypopituitarism.Grade 3: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization indicated

Hypogonadism.Definition: A disorder characterized by a reduction in serum testosterone in males and estrogen in females.
Hypogonadism.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Hypogonadism.Grade 2: Symptomatic (e.g., decreased libido, erectile dysfunction); intervention indicated
